codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the blocking FTS5 trigger bug from m1-sqlite-schema. Migration 002 drops the three buggy segment triggers (which used the memo's rowid as the FTS rowid) and recreates them using the segment's own rowid. Each segment now gets its own independent FTS entry. The insert trigger adds an FTS row with segment.rowid, the update trigger deletes the old FTS row and inserts a new one (both using segment.rowid), and the delete trigger removes the FTS row. The test coverage is thorough: test_fts5_basic_search was updated to rely on trigger-driven sync instead of manual FTS inserts, test_fts5_multi_segment_search verifies all 3 segments per memo are independently searchable, and test_fts5_segment_search_returns_correct_memo_id verifies the JOIN pattern for looking up memo_id from FTS results.

issues.description: Migration 001 still contains the buggy segment triggers. While migration 002 correctly drops and replaces them, the original buggy triggers remain in 001_initial_schema.sql. For a fresh database, both migrations run sequentially so this is functionally correct (002 drops the buggy triggers from 001 and replaces them). However, if someone reads only 001_initial_schema.sql, they'll see the buggy design with no indication it was fixed. This is acceptable since migrations should not be modified after being applied, and the migration runner ensures 002 always runs after 001.

The library doc still describes the old FTS5 trigger design without mentioning migration 002's fix. It says 'trg_segments_fts_insert/update/delete: Sync segment text changes' without clarifying that these now use segment.rowid (not memo.rowid). This could mislead future workers who reference the library doc.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library doc at .factory/library/sqlite-schema.md describes the FTS5 trigger design but doesn't reflect the fix in migration 002. It should be updated to document that segment triggers use segment.rowid (not memo.rowid) and that migration 002 exists to fix the original design. The FTS search pattern (JOIN segments ON s.rowid = f.rowid to get memo_id) is also not documented but is the intended query pattern for segment text search.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/sqlite-schema.md 'FTS5 Sync Triggers' section describes triggers without mentioning the rowid change; backend/tests/test_db.py:test_fts5_segment_search_returns_correct_memo_id demonstrates the JOIN pattern

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure correctly. The transcript shows proper TDD flow: baseline tests, implement fix, run tests, run quality checks, commit. SkillFeedback.followedProcedure = true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows sequential tool calls matching the skill procedure

summary: The fix adequately addresses the blocking issue from m1-sqlite-schema. The original bug was that segment FTS triggers used the memo's rowid, causing only the last segment per memo to be searchable. Migration 002 correctly drops those triggers and recreates them using each segment's own rowid, so all segments are independently indexed. The new tests directly validate the fix: multi-segment search confirms all segments are findable, and the memo_id lookup test validates the JOIN pattern for mapping FTS results back to memos. The updated test_fts5_basic_search also now tests trigger-driven sync instead of manual FTS inserts. PASS.
